content.text:
Module:
Specification Models for Distributed Systems  |
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF22
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Introduce students to the domain of formal design of parallel and distributed systems
Focus on specification formalisms and semantic models of parallelism
Introduce students to formal specification tools applied to concurrent, parallel and distributed systems during practical sessions
Recommended Prerequisites
Notions of software engineering
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Chapter 1:
Formal design methodology for parallel and distributed systems
Chapter 2:
Semantics and model of Labelled Transition Systems
Chapter 3: Petri Net Model
Syntax and semantics
Main characteristics
General properties of Petri nets
Modelling ingredients
Classic models (Producer-Consumer, Reader-Writer, Dining Philosophers)
Property analysis
Properties of Petri nets
Analysis via strongly connected components
Structural analysis of Petri nets
Chapter 3: The LOTOS Process Algebra
Basic LOTOS syntax
Data specification in LOTOS
Interleaved structured operational semantics of LOTOS
Classic formal specification examples
Chapter 4:
General overview of formal verification methods
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
Howard Bowman and Rodolfo Gomez, Concurrency Theory: Calculi and Automata for Modelling Untimed and Timed Concurrent Systems, 2006.
Manfred Broy et al., Model-Based Testing of Reactive Systems, Lecture Notes in Computer Science, Springer Berlin / Heidelberg, 2005.
Saidouni Djamel Eddine, Modèles du parallélisme, Polycopié (80 pages).
